causalmlr 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference

causalmlr: Causal Machine Learning with mlr3

Source: R/causalmlr-package.R
causalmlr-package.Rd

Estimation and evaluation of average and conditional average treatment effects (ATE/CATE) from observational data using machine learning, built on the mlr3 ecosystem.

ATE estimators

  • ate_naive() - difference in means (baseline)

  • ate_ipw() - inverse propensity weighting

  • ate_dr() - doubly robust / AIPW

  • ate_dml() - double machine learning (partially linear model)

CATE meta-learners

  • s_learner(), t_learner(), x_learner(), dr_learner()

Evaluation

  • eps_ate() - absolute ATE error (needs ground truth)

  • pehe() - precision in estimating heterogeneous effects (needs ground truth)

  • r_loss() with rloss_nuisance() - observable score for model selection and hyperparameter tuning on real data

All estimators accept arbitrary mlr3 learners as nuisance models, so anything from mlr3::lrn("regr.lm") to a tuned AutoTuner can be plugged in.

See also

Useful links:

  • https://github.com/dmachlanski/causalmlr

  • Report bugs at https://github.com/dmachlanski/causalmlr/issues
